descriptions.level_1: You reach the kitchen counter, grab a beige mug, and open a red tin of coffee.
descriptions.level_2: You stop in front of the counter next to the sink. You use your right hand to pick up a beige mug, set it down in front of you, and then use both hands to twist the red lid off a coffee tin.

Various kitchen items, including oils, a toaster, and an electric kettle, sit on the speckled, light-colored countertop. You reach out with your right hand to grab a simple beige mug, placing it firmly in the center of your workspace. Then, using both hands, you grab a red cylindrical tin of instant coffee and twist off its plastic lid, setting the lid down nearby.

descriptions.level_1: You pour instant coffee into the mug, then use an electric kettle to pour in hot water.
descriptions.level_2: Holding the red tin, you shake some instant coffee granules into the beige mug. You then set the tin down, grab the silver electric kettle to your right, and pour steaming water into the cup.

You set the tin aside on the left side of the mug. Reaching to your right, you grasp the black handle of a brushed stainless steel electric kettle. You lift the heavy kettle and carefully pour hot water into the mug, filling it close to the top.

descriptions.level_2: There is a quick cut showing you holding open the driver's side door of a car parked near the lake. Then, the scene jumps to a Shell gas station, where you are standing outside an open car door, reaching towards the hood of a black SUV.
descriptions.level_3: The view shifts rapidly. First, you are looking out from inside an open vehicle door parked right next to the lakeside path, holding the inner door handle. In the next instant, the setting completely changes to a Shell gas station on a sunny day. You are standing next to the open driver's door of a black SUV, reaching your right hand out towards the center of the vehicle's hood.

suggestions.text: Scene 4 (Car Door and Gas Station) appears slightly out of focus compared to other scenes — the car door handle and lake background are soft.
Scenes 1 and 3 both feature the lake prominently — you might consider placing them adjacent for visual continuity.
The lighting shifts noticeably from warm indoor tones in Scene 1 to bright outdoor exposure in Scene 2, creating an abrupt mood change.
Scene 5 (Opening SUV Hood) ends abruptly mid-action — the hood latch hasn't been fully released when the clip cuts.
The jump from lakeside walking (Scene 2) to approaching the water's edge (Scene 3) could use a brief transition or pause to signal the change in activity.
The jogger visible in Scene 2 adds dynamic movement but never reappears — consider whether this character element supports or distracts from your narrative.
There is a noticeable audio level difference between the indoor scene (Scene 1) and the outdoor scenes — the wind noise in Scenes 2-3 is much louder.
The black backpack appears in Scenes 1 and 2 as a connecting object but is not referenced again — this could be a strong visual thread to carry through the edit.
